test.command: pnpm --filter @workspace/radar-core run test && pnpm --filter @workspace/signal-atproto run test && pnpm --filter @riatzukiza/threat-radar-mcp run test && pnpm --filter @riatzukiza/threat-radar-web run test
test.details: radar-core: 55 tests (4 files), signal-atproto: 16 tests (2 files), threat-radar-mcp: 53 tests (4 files), threat-radar-web: 176 tests (11 files). Total: 300 tests passed, 0 failures.

typecheck.command: tsc --noEmit in radar-core, signal-atproto, threat-radar-mcp, threat-radar-web
typecheck.details: All 4 packages typecheck clean with zero errors.

nonBlockingIssues.description: No component tests added despite frontend-worker skill requiring them. Worker justified as 'layout-only refactor'.
RiskGauge gradient ID collision risk when multiple instances share the same label. ThreatClock lacks negative value guard.
Components define local TypeScript interfaces instead of importing from @workspace/radar-core, risking type drift.
No Zod validation on API response elements (AGENTS.md recommends Zod for external inputs). Low risk since API is local MCP server.
Dead code: aggregateSourceCounts() helper defined but never called. Hardcoded global domain tag filter list.
formatRelativeTime duplicated verbatim from EtaThreadCard.tsx. Should extract to shared utility.

Unused imports (applyWeights, computeCompositeScore) in App.tsx. Weight logic duplicated between EtaLane inline and usePersonalization utility. No tests for CriticalThinkingSection or ActionFeed components.
Weights only flow to η lane gauges. μ lane does not receive or apply personalization weights.
Firehose operates at thread-level granularity rather than raw SignalEvent level. No entry count cap for large datasets.

appliedUpdates.description: Added testing patterns to architecture.md: (1) vitest fake-timer + waitFor conflict resolution using flushPromises() helper, (2) threat-radar-web uses vitest 1.6.x with -t flag instead of --grep.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: frontend-worker skill: Enforce or clarify agent-browser visual verification requirement. 3 of 7 dashboard-mvp workers skipped step 5 (visual verification via agent-browser) with no skill deviation recorded. Either make it mandatory with clearer language, or explicitly mark it optional when no new visual components are added.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: dashboard-3-lane-layout, clock-gauge-components, and personalization-controls workers all skipped agent-browser verification. Only eta-lane-signals and firehose-empty-error-states performed visual checks.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: shared UI utilities should live in src/ui/utils/ or src/ui/helpers/. formatRelativeTime is duplicated across EtaThreadCard.tsx and MuThreadCard.tsx. Data-fetching hooks go in src/api/, UI state hooks in src/ui/hooks/.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mu-lane-signals review found formatRelativeTime duplicated verbatim. personalization-controls created src/ui/hooks/ for usePersonalization while useRadarPolling lives in src/api/. No documented convention for either.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: centralize lane-to-tag category mappings. η lane filters by ['geopolitical','infrastructure','economic','security','climate'], μ lane by ['local','community','oss','developer','open-source','ai']. These hardcoded lists in separate components will diverge. Document them in one place or extract to a shared config.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: eta-lane-signals (EtaLane.tsx:77) and mu-lane-signals (MuLane.tsx:34) each independently define which domain_tags qualify for their lane. No shared constant or mapping exists.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: frontend-worker skill: Add note that minimal backend changes (1-5 lines) to existing endpoints are acceptable when they directly enable the UI feature, rather than requiring return-to-orchestrator for a separate backend feature.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: eta-lane-signals worker pragmatically added 2 lines to threat-radar-mcp main.ts to include threads in the API response, rather than returning to orchestrator. This was the right call but deviated from skill guidance.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: CSS class prefix pattern. Each component area uses a short prefix (lane-, pers-, ct-, af-, fh-) for CSS classes. Document this BEM-like convention to maintain consistency.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All 7 features consistently used prefixed CSS classes (lane-header, pers-panel, ct-section, af-feed, fh-panel) but the pattern is nowhere documented.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker used `as React.CSSProperties` type cast for CSS custom properties. Suggested documenting as convention.
rejectedObservations.reason: Standard React pattern for CSS custom properties. Too minor to warrant AGENTS.md documentation.

rejectedObservations.observation: Private sub-component pattern (defining sub-components in the same file) not documented.
rejectedObservations.reason: Already established pattern across the codebase. Consistent usage makes explicit documentation unnecessary.

rejectedObservations.observation: vitest --grep flag discrepancy in features.json verificationSteps.
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented in architecture.md Testing Notes section. Feature definition issue, not a codebase convention gap.
Already noted in architecture.md. Version difference is acceptable since threat-radar-web has a separate vite config and vitest 1.x is backward compatible for test APIs.
